info.name: Gestlio – Payment (Stripe Connect Escrow)
info.description:
Stripe Connect escrow payments (USD, multi-country).

Flow:
1) Cleaner onboards a Connect payout account (connect/onboard -> open URL).
2) Cleaner accepts a schedule (status 'accepted').
3) Host pays -> funds HELD in the platform balance (escrow).
4) Cleaner does the work + submits proof.
5) Host approves via PATCH /schedule/:id/complete -> 95% auto-transferred to the cleaner, platform keeps 5%.

The webhook (POST /payment/webhook) is called by Stripe, not from Postman. Use the Stripe CLI:
  stripe listen --forward-to localhost:6050/api/v1/payment/webhook

Set collection variables:
- baseUrl (e.g. http://localhost:6050/api/v1)
- cleanerToken, hostToken, adminToken (JWTs)
- scheduleId, paymentId

request.description: country is ISO-2 (US, FR, GB...). Returns { url, accountId }. Open url in a browser to finish Stripe Express onboarding. NOTE: Stripe does not support payouts to all countries (e.g. Bangladesh is not supported).
Refreshes from Stripe. Returns { onboarded, payoutsEnabled, detailsSubmitted }. payoutsEnabled must be true before a host can pay.

Schedule must be 'accepted' and the cleaner must have payoutsEnabled. Returns { paymentId, paymentIntentClientSecret, ephemeralKey, customerId, publishableKey, amount, currency }.

Confirm the payment client-side:
- Mobile: Stripe PaymentSheet with customerId + ephemeralKey + paymentIntentClientSecret + publishableKey.
- Web: Stripe.js / Elements with paymentIntentClientSecret + publishableKey.
Test card: 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future date, any CVC.
Refunds a payment that is still 'paid_held' (escrow) back to the host.
This is the existing schedule endpoint. Blocked (400) unless paymentStatus = paid_held. On success it transfers 95% to the cleaner's Connect account and marks the payment 'released'.
